conclusion: Original identification of the -13910 (GRCh38 forward-strand G>A) variant fully co-segregating with adult-type hypolactasia/lactase persistence in Finnish families.

Extended haplotype homozygosity analysis found a strong signature of recent positive selection centered on the persistence allele, consistent with a historical selective advantage in dairying populations.

Reporter-gene assay showed the persistence-associated allele significantly increases lactase (LCT) promoter/enhancer activity relative to the ancestral allele, providing mechanistic support for the association.
Review of the evolutionary genetics and worldwide distribution of lactase persistence variants, including rs4988235 in Europeans and additional variants identified in African populations.
Characterized as part of the persistence-associated haplotype alongside the -13910 (rs4988235) variant in the original Finnish family study.
Localized the causal lactase persistence element to a 1 Mb linkage-disequilibrium block in Europeans encompassing both rs182549 and rs4988235; rs182549 tags the same persistence haplotype.
Selection-signature analysis at the LCT locus included this linked marker within the extended haplotype under recent positive selection.
Review discussing rs182549 as one of the markers historically used to track the European lactase persistence haplotype.
